things.name: Blarney Castle & Stone
things.description: Medieval castle where kissing the famous stone is said to bestow the gift of eloquence

things.name: English Market
things.description: Covered food market in operation since 1788, visited by Queen Elizabeth II in 2011

things.name: Cork City Gaol
things.description: Fascinating 19th-century prison museum telling stories of hardship and survival

things.name: Crawford Art Gallery
things.description: Free public art gallery with a superb collection of Irish and European art

things.name: St Fin Barre's Cathedral
things.description: Stunning French Gothic Revival cathedral with intricate stonework

things.name: Cobh Heritage Centre
things.description: Titanic's last port of call with the story of Irish emigration

things.name: Spike Island
things.description: Island fortress and former prison with guided tours in Cork Harbour

things.name: Elizabeth Fort
things.description: 17th-century star-shaped fortress with panoramic views over the city

things.name: Shandon Bells & Tower
things.description: Iconic 18th-century church tower where visitors can ring the famous bells

things.name: Kinsale
things.description: Picturesque harbour town just south of Cork with gourmet restaurants and historic forts
